test_cases.title: Successful OAuth redirect to provider authorization page
preconditions: At least one supported third-party OAuth provider is configured and available
User is on the sign-in page
steps: Identify a supported OAuth provider listed on the sign-in page
Click the sign-in button for that provider
test_cases.expected_result: The user is redirected to the selected OAuth provider's authorization page (URL domain matches the known provider's authorization endpoint).

test_cases.title: New account created and user redirected to dashboard after first-time OAuth authorization
preconditions: No existing account is linked to the OAuth identity being used
A supported OAuth provider is available
steps: Click the sign-in button for a supported OAuth provider
Complete authorization on the provider's authorization page with credentials that have no existing linked account
Allow the provider to return the authorization callback to the system
test_cases.expected_result: A new user account is created in the system and the user is redirected to their dashboard.

test_cases.title: Existing linked account user is logged in and redirected to dashboard after OAuth authorization
preconditions: An account already exists in the system linked to the OAuth identity being used
A supported OAuth provider is available
steps: Click the sign-in button for the supported OAuth provider that is linked to an existing account
Complete authorization on the provider's authorization page using credentials linked to the existing account
Allow the provider to return the authorization callback to the system
test_cases.expected_result: The user is logged into the existing linked account and redirected to their dashboard, with no duplicate account created.

test_cases.title: User denies authorization on OAuth provider page — system handles error gracefully
preconditions: A supported OAuth provider is available
User is on the sign-in page
steps: Click the sign-in button for a supported OAuth provider
On the provider's authorization page, choose to deny or cancel the authorization request
Allow the provider to return the denial callback to the system
test_cases.expected_result: The user is not logged in or registered, and the system returns them to the sign-in page or displays an appropriate error message without creating a new account.

test_cases.title: OAuth callback with tampered or invalid state parameter is rejected
preconditions: A supported OAuth provider is available
steps: Initiate the OAuth sign-in flow for a supported provider
Intercept the authorization callback URL before it reaches the system
Modify the 'state' parameter to an arbitrary or mismatched value
Submit the tampered callback URL to the system
test_cases.expected_result: The system rejects the callback, does not create or authenticate any account, and returns an error response indicating an invalid or mismatched state.

test_cases.title: Unsupported OAuth provider sign-in attempt is not processed
preconditions: An OAuth provider not in the supported providers list is identified
steps: Attempt to initiate an OAuth sign-in flow by crafting or navigating to a request referencing an unsupported provider (e.g., via a manipulated URL parameter)
test_cases.expected_result: The system does not redirect to the unsupported provider and returns an error or ignores the request without exposing internal details.

assumptions: The system uses a 'state' parameter or equivalent CSRF protection mechanism during the OAuth flow.
The sign-in page presents distinct UI controls for each supported OAuth provider.
The user's dashboard is a defined, accessible page immediately after authentication.
Account linking is determined by matching a unique identifier returned by the provider (e.g., provider-specific user ID or verified email).
The system is capable of receiving OAuth callbacks at a known redirect URI registered with each provider.
notes: The requirement does not specify: (1) which specific OAuth providers are supported — test cases reference 'a supported provider' generically; (2) behavior when the provider returns an error other than user denial (e.g., provider downtime, invalid client credentials) — no test case covers this; (3) whether account merging is supported if the same email exists via a different provider or password-based sign-in — edge cases around duplicate email identity linking are not covered; (4) session management details post-login (e.g., token expiry, session duration) are not testable from this requirement; (5) the exact appearance or URL of the dashboard is not specified, limiting precision in the expected result for redirect assertions.
